page: Microsoft Azure F2as_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Fasv6 server family, powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs utilizing an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to external high-performance storage volumes.

In benchmark testing, the AMD EPYC processor demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier (top 10%) and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25%. However, multi-core performance is more constrained, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores falling into the average and bottom 25% categories respectively. Memory bandwidth tests reveal lower performance, with read-only small and large block bandwidths ranking in the bottom 25%, though write bandwidth for large blocks remains average. The instance performs well on single-threaded tasks such as bzip3 decompression and text processing, but shows weaker results in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F2as_v6 offers dedicated CPU resources at a lower entry point, making it a viable option for workloads that require consistent single-core CPU performance without the overhead of high memory capacity. Due to the lack of local storage and modest memory-to-CPU ratio, it is best suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads, single-threaded processing tasks, basic web serving, and small-scale application hosting where high memory bandwidth is not a critical requirement.
description: Microsoft Azure F2as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it includes 8.0 GB of system memory and is Premium Storage capable, though it lacks local storage and bundled GPUs. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core results in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Conversely, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, placing in the bottom 25%. This resource profile makes the F2as_v6 highly suitable for single-threaded processing, lightweight application hosting, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from dedicated, high-clock-speed CPU cores but do not demand high memory density or local storage.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F2as_v6 is a 2-vCPU virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 8.0 GB of RAM, and dedicated cores, delivering strong single-core performance for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F2as_v6 is a 2-vCPU virtual machine with an AMD EPYC 9V74 CPU and 8.0 GB of RAM, optimized for single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core virtual machine featuring high single-core clock speeds and balanced memory for lightweight, compute-focused workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs
Equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory and 32 MB of L3 cache
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or bundled GPUs
Best suited for single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, and basic application hosting
